purpose: Refresh the frozen Stage4 official-field scanner, resolve the distinction between short local hourly OI imports and long official daily OI history, and test whether apparent 180-day API windows can be paginated backward before registering any new candidate.

margin_loan_ratio.conclusion: The public route exposes a rolling 180-row daily window under the tested parameters; no backward history route was found.

taker_volume_aggregate.conclusion: The public route exposes a rolling 180-row daily window under the tested parameters and remains below the frozen 365-day gate.

insurance_fund.conclusion: Backward paging exists, but the field is a system-level insurance-fund state rather than a mature per-contract cross-section; it also belongs to closed liquidation/insurance-pressure rare-event families. No bulk history collection is authorized.

liquidation_orders.conclusion: Recent liquidation records can page backward, but the route is short-window, event-driven and directly duplicates the closed forced-flow/liquidation family. No data download or candidate registration is allowed.

state_correction.superseded_statement: Native OI is simply waiting for more data.

correct_distinction.local_imported_hourly_oi: Short and uneven 16-base archive from roughly 2026-04 to 2026-06; not the authoritative reason for closing the family.

state_correction.current_decision: Do not wait for more daily OI history. I023-I025 are closed because their price-plus-OI continuation, reversal and deleveraging mechanisms hit FP12 and prior momentum/reversal/liquidation duplicates, with cost scale or direction not independently identified before PnL.

primary_evidence.title: Reconciling Open Interest with Traded Volume in Perpetual Swaps
primary_evidence.use: Supports caution about exchange OI measurement and leverage interpretation; it does not establish a unique cross-sectional return direction from OI.

primary_evidence.title: Explainable Patterns in Cryptocurrency Microstructure
primary_evidence.use: Predictive microstructure evidence relies on continuous one-second order-book and trade features, not daily aggregate taker or OI fields.

primary_evidence.title: Fundamentals of Perpetual Futures
primary_evidence.use: Provides a theoretical basis for perpetual/spot/funding convergence, a family already closed in this project; it does not create an independent OI Alpha.

allowed_next_work: continue H22, V357 and H27 prospective evidence updates
retain mature official daily OI as a closed-family diagnostic control rather than waiting for more data

passively recheck the rolling 180-day and 72-day official routes only when the API itself exposes materially longer history; do not collect duplicate daily snapshots expecting the public window to grow
search for a genuinely different OKX point-in-time field with a direct per-contract payer and unique ex-ante direction
